candidates.stmt: Originally, from Melbourne Australia, Kevinarrived in Southampton on a passenger ship in 1973 on his quest to fulill a childhood dream of seeing the world. Following his marriage to Hazel, they eventually settled in Alverstoke in 1984 and have two daughters. Kevin's working career started in Australia as a civil engineering draftsman. His travels eventually lead him to a 4o-year career in the offshore oil industry working all round the world as a professional diver. He retired in 2016 and Hazel recently retired from a long career in the NHS. History and heritage is Kevin's passion, so Gosport was the perfect place for him to settle. Due to his love of Stokes Bay, he has served on the Friends of Stokes Bay committee for 27 years and was recently appointed as Chair. He also serves on the Gosport Society Committee,  Kevin has been the Diving Museum director since it opened in 2011 and serves on the Advisory Panel for the Southeast Museums Development and the steering committee for the Hampshire Military Museums Network.  Kevin is GBC Heritage Champion, Vive Vhair of the Regulatory Board and member of the Economic Development and Policy and organisation Boards.

STEVE HAMMOND has been the Councillor for Bridgemary North since 2017. He lives within the Bridgemary North Ward and is married with two adult children and one grandchild. A Gosport resident since 1962 Steve worked in the telecommunications sector and is now retired. Steve is a member of St. Mary’s junior sailing club where he teaches 9-15 year old children to sail. He is also a member of the Lords Taverners where he has helped disabled children play table cricket. Steve also hosts 10 Facebook business pages where local firms can promote their businesses for free.

ROB HYLANDS has been a Brockhurst Ward Councillor since 2009 and is currently the Deputy Leader of the Liberal Democrat Group. Married to Lorna, Rob is the landlord of The Foresters Arms public house in Anns Hill Road, Gosport. He enjoys most sports from darts to football and is a keen supporter of Gosport Borough FC. In his spare time he enjoys walking his two small dogs and supporting local community organisations and events.

I grew up between Hull and Gosport. When I moved back in 2024 with my family, I had fond memories of a thriving high street full of energy and community. Coming back it's clear that Gosport hasn't had the investment it deserves.

As a young, working-class person, I don't just see the statistics - I live them. I know the struggles my family and neighbours face with the cost of living and housing. Things are tough right now, but they don't have to stay this way.

I'm standing for Brockhurst and Privett because every part of Gosport deserves a voice - and a councillor who will show up, listen, and fight for the investment this ward needs.

Antony is a dedicated community campaigner for the Gosport Reform UK branch and played an active role in the recent campaign supporting Councillor Paul Carter. His commitment to Gosport is rooted in a lifetime of local involvement and a strong belief that positive change starts at community level.
​
Born in Portsmouth and raised in Gosport, Antony has deep and lasting ties to the area. From an early age, he has believed in stepping up and getting involved. His experience spans youth and school councils, organising school fundraising events, and serving as a governor at two local schools. These roles have given him a clear understanding of the challenges facing families, young people and local services, as well as the importance of strong leadership and accountability.
​
Antony stands with Reform UK because he believes politics must return to honesty, responsibility and common sense. He is passionate about protecting taxpayers’ money, cutting waste and bureaucracy, and ensuring decisions are made locally, not imposed from afar. He is a strong advocate for safeguarding vital services such as education and policing, which form the backbone of a safe and thriving community.
​
His priority is straightforward: to listen to residents, stand up for Gosport, and work tirelessly to deliver real, practical improvements that make a genuine difference to everyday life.

I have lived in the ward, with my young family, since 2014. In that time I have been a proud member of the community, leading local events and supporting causes, all the while listening to residents’ concerns. My background is in IT solutions for charities and I intend to use that experience to serve Grange and Alver Valley as its councillor.

We have a fantastic piece of Gosport here. The beautiful Alver Valley on our doorstep, and the most genuine people you could hope to meet. We are also unfairly overlooked when it comes to handing out funding, which is why I got involved in local politics. For over a decade our representatives have done a poor job in standing up for our area and it is time that changed.

Gosport Labour is on your side. We rise above childish political games to deliver on the priorities of Gosport’s residents.

I am passionate about ensuring the residents of Forton and Gosport are heard and represented. As part of my community work, I am the Chair of Gosport Heritage Open Days, the coordinator of The Centre which assists rough sleepers, veterans and vulnerable people and the coordinator of Gosport Speedwatch to keep our roads safe. Married with two children, family is very important to me and I am keen to improve our area for all ages.

I have called Gosport home my entire life. I’ve seen our town change over the years, but my commitment to it has never wavered.

As a mother of four and a foster carer of 10 years, my life has been defined by looking after the next generation and has given me a valuable insight into the challenges facing our local services and the families that rely on them.

I am standing for the Green Party because I want to look after our town’s future. I will fight for better local services, affordable housing and a fairer community for everyone.

I served as a Conservative councillor for 8 years in Woking. I later stood for the Conservatives in Brockhurst Ward in the Gosport Council elections of 2018. I joined The British Union and Sovereignty Party in 2019 and fought a by-election in Brockhurst gaining a very impressive 165 Votes or 17% of the vote which is very good for a lone campaigner. My campaign was a call to get Brexit done and defend the Union which proved popular in a strong Leave area. I returned to The Conservative Party in 2022 after encouragement from local people who made me realise that I still had a great deal to offer my area and Borough.

Worked in IT for 40+ years , in the public and private sector. A qualified economist and systems implementation specialist . Also an member of Greenpeace since 1985, as well as the Green Party here in Hampshire and in Cheshire Moved to Gosport last year and believe that we have four areas we need to tackle 1. A more equal distribution of the rewards of our economic system. This will reduce inequality and opens up opportunity for everyone to succeed. There is plenty of research that shows making a society more equal makes it a happier place 2. Finland was ranking the happiest country in the world in 2023 -- why ? because their welfare system's ability to help its citizens feel taken care of," Things like relatively generous unemployment benefits and nearly free healthcare help mitigate sources of unhappiness, 3. Push for the move to a greener economy to tackle climate change AND give us cleaner energy , reduced pollution making our environment cleaner and quieter and remove the need for dirty fossil energy , aiming for a safe and healthy Gosport 4. I heard a commentator on the radio today say 'we can't afford Net Zero' -- really we need to wake up Gosport to because the costs of not doing Net Zero area LOT higher If I had to have a slogan, it would be ' Doing Nothing is not an option -- for you , me and 9 billion others on planet Earth -- we need to start here ..

Graham and his wife Mal have lived in Lee on the Solent for over 40 years. He moved here with his family whilst serving in the Royal Navy where he served for 24 years both above and below the waves. Graham has been a Gosport Borough Councillor since 1998 but most recently worked closely with fellow Councillors, local organisations and volunteers to make LEE HUB a reality, ensuring that we do not lose our library.

A proud Gosport resident for over 20 years, I have built my life here, raising a family and supporting our vibrant local businesses. I am dedicated to creating a fairer community for everyone and working together to ensure a safer, greener environment for our future.

Marie grew up in Lee, attending Lee Junior school and then secondary school in Waterlooville before leaving to go to university. She returned to live in Lee 22 years ago

She recently retired after a career working as a statistician, initially in research and then 25 years as a civil servant.

Marie volunteers locally and for Statisticians for Society, a project connecting statisticians and charities. She is concerned about the local environment and the erosion of public services.

DAWN KELLY has been a Christchurch Ward Councillor since first being elected in 2016. Dawn has lived in Gosport for most of her life and currently resides within the Christchurch Ward at 77 Kings Road, Gosport. Hailing from a Royal Naval family Dawn has travelled to many countries over the years but always returned to Gosport. A keen allotment gardener, Dawn currently serves on Gosport Council’s Community; Economic Development; and Regulatory Boards.

I have lived in and been an active part of my local community in Leesland and Newtown for many years. Caring for local green spaces, working with local young people and volunteering, hoping to create more pride in where we live. I enjoy many of the creative arts Gosport has to offer and would love to see more. Working from personal experiences, I'm keen to highlight the many local issues which affect people with disabilities and mental health including the lack of doctors and dentists. I believe in helping by informing, signposting, networking and working together with residents and the many great charities, businesses, community groups and services to enhance the lives of those living in Gosport. Alongside this I am passionate about improving our local transport, reducing waste, promoting recycling and, conserving and improving our green spaces. I want to help businesses, the council and individuals implement green alternatives. 
Giving Leesland and Newtown Ward a Green voice.

Hilary has a long association with Grange, regularly gets involved in conservation projects and runs a children’s environmental group from Grange Farm 1990. She continues to work in Early Years education and organises collections for the local foodbank.

Conservative cuts mean that Gosport Council gets 40% less funding than it did in 2010. The evidence is everywhere: from schools to street lights, from play parks to policing, libraries closed, fewer flower beds, streets cleaned less often. Like most residents, she is especially concerned about the recent rise in crimes, such as drug-dealing and anti-social behaviour.

I believe that a local councillor can make a huge difference. After 29 years representing our area, I still have the same passion, drive, and determination to improve the quality of life for the people of Peel Common and Rowner. They know that I will stand up for them, as I always have, regardless of party politics and that I will continue to do everything I can to defend our area against unwanted major developments.

Jess grew up in Gosport and was one of the inaugural intake of students at St Vincent College. He has a degree in History from the University of London.

Jess is Chief Executive of the God's Port Housing Society. He is involved in several local leisure and charitable organisations and was Mayor's Escort of Gosport in 2006/07.

DAVID HERRIDGE is seeking re-election as a local Councillor having represented Leesland on Gosport Council since 2018. Born in Stubbington he has lived in the Gosport area all his life. After doing an engineering apprenticeship at British Rail he currently works at Pall Corporation in Portsmouth. David is a keen sports fan and supporter of Gosport Borough FC. He enjoys live music and real ales in local pubs. As a Councillor he led the community campaign to stop over-development of the former ‘Buildbase’ site in Whitworth Road and remains committed to protecting local sites from insensitive over-development.

I currently represent Hardway as Ward Councillor, with a total 9 year experience.  My family is important and helping with grandchildren a priority, but Gosport is a large part of my life, having always lived here and I am always ready to listen to, support and try to help, whether as Councillor, friend, or colleague. My voting record shows I vote positively and haven’t missed any Council Boards or Meetings in the last five years.

Claire has worked for a local community charity for over a decade and works hard to ensure children and their families are supported to lead happy healthy lives. Claire has been an active volunteer in Gosport since childhood. She is currently Vice Chair of the Friends of Leesland Park, helps run a children's environmental club and is a member of her daughter's school PTA. Claire lives in Gosport with her husband and young daughter and is passionate about making Gosport a great place for everyone to live.
